test_plan.strategy: Reductive feature — the test suite verifies that (a) the dispatcher recognises only the 4 allowed CLI inputs, (b) the deleted modules and their tests are gone and the remaining suite still passes, (c) the React empty-state no longer mentions CLI and links to /admin, (d) documentation contains no residual CLI references, (e) a legacy projects.json (with defaultInterface) is read unchanged, (f) the existing /api/projects routes continue to work. Test levels are assigned per the Type Coverage Matrix. Unit tests exercise the dispatcher, the empty-state React branch, and docs-grep gates. Integration tests hit the filesystem for projects.json compatibility and spawn the `aitri-hub` binary as a subprocess. E2E tests load the rendered SPA in a Playwright browser to assert the empty-state CTA target and 768px layout.

type_coverage_matrix.reason: type UX → E2E always MUST; CLI surface is exercised by subprocess (integration) and help-string inspection (unit)
type persistence → Integration MUST (filesystem verification of deletions); unit covers grep-over-source
type UX → E2E MUST; unit covers the React JSX branch; no integration layer applies
type reporting (docs) → fully verifiable via grep in unit tests; no runtime surface to integrate
type persistence → Integration MUST (real file I/O with legacy fixture)
type logic → Unit MUST... but the logic is in an HTTP handler; integration covers it. Parent project already has the Playwright e2e admin-api suite which is reused here

test_cases.title: aitri-hub help lists only the 4 allowed commands
preconditions: bin/aitri-hub.js exists and is executable under node >=18
test_cases.given: a clean checkout of the post-feature tree at process.cwd() and NO environment variable overrides
test_cases.when: a child process runs `node bin/aitri-hub.js help` and stdout is captured as a UTF-8 string
test_cases.then: stdout contains the literal substrings 'aitri-hub web', 'integration review', 'aitri-hub help', '--version' AND stdout contains zero occurrences of each of 'aitri-hub monitor', 'aitri-hub setup', 'aitri-hub init' AND exit code is 0
steps: spawn child process: node bin/aitri-hub.js help
assert all four positive substrings present
assert all three negative substrings absent
test_cases.expected_result: Exit 0, stdout contains the 4 required tokens and none of the 3 forbidden tokens

test_cases.title: aitri-hub monitor exits non-zero with 'Unknown command'
test_cases.given: a clean checkout at process.cwd()
test_cases.when: a child process runs `node bin/aitri-hub.js monitor` and stdout/stderr are captured
test_cases.then: exit code is exactly 1 AND stderr contains the substring "Unknown command: 'monitor'" AND stderr contains the substring "Run 'aitri-hub help' for usage." AND stdout does NOT contain 'Dashboard running'
steps: wait for exit (no timeout — the process must terminate on its own)
assert stderr includes "Run 'aitri-hub help' for usage."
assert stdout does not include 'Dashboard running'
test_cases.expected_result: Exit 1; stderr contains the unknown-command message

test_cases.title: aitri-hub setup exits non-zero with 'Unknown command'
test_cases.given: a clean checkout at process.cwd()
test_cases.when: a child process runs `node bin/aitri-hub.js setup`
test_cases.then: exit code === 1 AND stderr contains "Unknown command: 'setup'"
test_cases.expected_result: Exit 1; stderr contains unknown-command message for 'setup'

test_cases.title: aitri-hub init exits non-zero with 'Unknown command'
test_cases.given: a clean checkout at process.cwd()
test_cases.when: a child process runs `node bin/aitri-hub.js init` with stdin piped from /dev/null (to prove no wizard hangs)
test_cases.then: exit code === 1 AND stderr contains "Unknown command: 'init'" AND the process terminates within 2000ms (proves no readline prompt blocks)
steps: spawn: node bin/aitri-hub.js init with stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe']
test_cases.expected_result: Exit 1 within 2 seconds; stderr contains unknown-command for 'init'

test_cases.title: aitri-hub with no args prints usage and exits 0
test_cases.given: a clean checkout at process.cwd()
test_cases.when: a child process runs `node bin/aitri-hub.js` with no subcommand
test_cases.then: exit code === 0 AND stdout contains the substring 'aitri-hub web' AND stdout contains 'Usage:'
test_cases.expected_result: Exit 0; usage banner printed

test_cases.title: aitri-hub --version prints semver and exits 0
preconditions: package.json version is a valid semver string
test_cases.given: package.json contains a `version` field matching /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/
test_cases.when: a child process runs `node bin/aitri-hub.js --version`
test_cases.then: exit code === 0 AND stdout trimmed matches /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/
test_cases.expected_result: Exit 0; stdout is the package version

test_cases.title: aitri-hub integration review records the manifest for a valid version
preconditions: a CHANGELOG.md fixture exists in a temp dir with a section for version 0.1.80
test_cases.given: a tmpdir containing CHANGELOG.md with a heading '## 0.1.80' followed by at least one non-empty line, and AITRI_HUB_DIR pointing to a separate tmpdir
test_cases.when: a child process runs `node bin/aitri-hub.js integration review 0.1.80 --changelog <tmpdir>/CHANGELOG.md`
test_cases.then: exit code === 0 AND <AITRI_HUB_DIR>/integration-compat.json exists AND its parsed JSON has reviewedUpTo === '0.1.80' AND changelogHash is a 64-char hex string
steps: spawn: node bin/aitri-hub.js integration review 0.1.80 --changelog tmpdir1/CHANGELOG.md, env: { AITRI_HUB_DIR: tmpdir2 }
test_cases.expected_result: Manifest written with the correct version and a 64-hex SHA-256 hash

test_cases.title: aitri-hub integration review with malformed version exits with INVALID_VERSION
test_cases.given: a clean checkout
test_cases.when: a child process runs `node bin/aitri-hub.js integration review not-a-version`
test_cases.then: exit code === 2 AND stderr contains the substring 'invalid version'
steps: spawn: node bin/aitri-hub.js integration review not-a-version
test_cases.expected_result: Exit 2; invalid-version error reported

test_cases.title: Deleted command modules are absent from the tree
preconditions: tests run from the repository root (process.cwd())
test_cases.given: the post-feature repository checked out at process.cwd()
test_cases.when: the test reads the filesystem for paths lib/commands/init.js, lib/commands/setup.js, lib/commands/monitor.js using fs.existsSync
test_cases.then: all three fs.existsSync calls return false AND fs.existsSync('lib/commands/web.js') returns true AND fs.existsSync('lib/commands/integration-review.js') returns true
test_cases.expected_result: Exactly web.js and integration-review.js remain under lib/commands/

test_cases.title: No source file imports a deleted command
test_cases.given: the post-feature repository at process.cwd()
test_cases.when: a recursive grep is run for the regex 'from .*commands/(init|setup|monitor)' across bin/, lib/, tests/, and web/
test_cases.then: the grep returns zero matches
steps: for each of ['bin','lib','tests','web']: recursively read every .js/.jsx file
for each file: test the regex /from ['"].*commands\/(init|setup|monitor)/
test_cases.expected_result: No residual imports of deleted modules

test_cases.title: Deleted test files are absent from the tree
test_cases.given: the post-feature repository
test_cases.when: fs.existsSync is called for tests/unit/monitor-stub.test.js and tests/integration/setup.test.js
test_cases.then: both return false
test_cases.expected_result: Obsolete test files are gone

test_cases.title: bin/aitri-hub.js source contains no reference to cmdInit/cmdSetup/cmdMonitor
test_cases.given: post-feature bin/aitri-hub.js on disk
test_cases.when: the file is read as a UTF-8 string and the regex /cmdInit|cmdSetup|cmdMonitor/ is evaluated
test_cases.then: the regex returns null (no match)
test_cases.expected_result: No dead symbol references remain in the dispatcher

test_cases.title: OverviewTab empty state renders a link to /admin
preconditions: React Testing Library or equivalent JSX renderer available in web/ tests
test_cases.given: OverviewTab is mounted with props { projects: [], loading: false }
test_cases.when: the rendered DOM is queried for an anchor or button whose accessible target resolves to '/admin'
test_cases.then: exactly one such element is found AND its accessible name contains the substring 'Add your first project'
steps: query DOM for elements with href='/admin' or data-href='/admin'
assert matches[0].textContent includes 'Add your first project'
test_cases.expected_result: Single /admin CTA with the expected label

test_cases.title: OverviewTab empty state does not mention removed CLI commands
test_cases.given: OverviewTab mounted with projects=[], loading=false
test_cases.when: the rendered HTML body string is produced (via renderToStaticMarkup or equivalent)
test_cases.then: the HTML string contains zero occurrences of the substrings 'aitri-hub setup', 'aitri-hub monitor', 'aitri-hub init', 'docker compose'
steps: for each forbidden substring: assert html.indexOf(substring) === -1
test_cases.expected_result: No residual CLI references in the empty-state DOM

test_cases.title: Empty-state panel has no horizontal scroll at 768px viewport
preconditions: aitri-hub web running against an empty ~/.aitri-hub/ (tmpdir)
test_cases.given: AITRI_HUB_DIR points to an empty tmpdir and `aitri-hub web` is started on a free port
test_cases.when: a Playwright browser context with viewport 768x900 navigates to http://localhost:<port>/ and waits for networkidle
test_cases.then: document.documentElement.scrollWidth === document.documentElement.clientWidth AND a DOM query for `a[href='/admin']` returns exactly one node
steps: launch Playwright chromium, newContext({ viewport: { width: 768, height: 900 } })
teardown: SIGINT the server, remove tmpdir
test_cases.expected_result: No horizontal scroll, exactly one /admin CTA visible

test_cases.title: aitri-hub web starts without blocking when projects.json is absent
test_cases.given: AITRI_HUB_DIR set to a fresh tmpdir with no projects.json, AITRI_HUB_PORT set to a free port, stdin closed
test_cases.when: aitri-hub web is spawned with stdio:['ignore','pipe','pipe'] and we poll GET http://localhost:<port>/health
test_cases.then: within 5000ms, GET /health returns status 200 with body 'ok\n' AND the child process is still alive (exit code === null)
steps: spawn: node bin/aitri-hub.js web with env and stdin closed
poll GET /health every 100ms for up to 5000ms
SIGINT child and wait for clean exit
test_cases.expected_result: Server answers /health in <5s and does not block on stdin

test_cases.title: README / DEPLOYMENT contain no residual CLI references
test_cases.given: the post-feature README.md and DEPLOYMENT.md at the repo root
test_cases.when: each file is read and the regex /aitri-hub (monitor|setup|init)(?!\-)/ is evaluated over its content
test_cases.then: for each file, regex.test(content) === false (zero matches across both files combined)
test_cases.expected_result: Zero occurrences of deprecated CLI commands in primary docs

test_cases.title: README Quick Start lists npm install and aitri-hub web in order
test_cases.given: README.md at the repo root
test_cases.when: the file content is parsed and the first fenced bash block after a heading matching /^##\s+Quick\s*Start/i is extracted
test_cases.then: the extracted block contains the lines 'npm install' and 'aitri-hub web' in that order AND does NOT contain 'aitri-hub setup', 'aitri-hub monitor', 'docker compose'
steps: find the Quick Start heading position
from that position, find the next ```bash block
test_cases.expected_result: Quick Start is 2 lines: npm install, aitri-hub web

test_cases.title: DEPLOYMENT.md wraps Docker instructions under an 'Optional' heading
test_cases.given: DEPLOYMENT.md at the repo root
test_cases.when: the file is scanned for an ATX heading (level 2 or 3) whose text contains the case-insensitive substring 'optional' and the tokens 'Docker' or 'docker'
test_cases.then: at least one such heading exists AND all mentions of `docker compose up` in the file occur after that heading line number
steps: record line number of first match as L_opt
find all lines containing 'docker compose up'
assert every such line has line number > L_opt
test_cases.expected_result: All Docker instructions live under an 'Optional: Docker' section

test_cases.title: README no longer describes a CLI dashboard mode
test_cases.given: README.md at the repo root
test_cases.when: the file is read and searched for the substrings 'CLI terminal dashboard' and 'aitri-hub monitor' (case-sensitive)
test_cases.then: both .indexOf calls return -1
test_cases.expected_result: README.md contains neither phrase

test_cases.title: Legacy projects.json (with defaultInterface) reads all entries unchanged
test_cases.given: a tmpdir as AITRI_HUB_DIR containing projects.json with content { version: 1, defaultInterface: 'cli', projects: [ { id: 'a', name: 'Alpha', type: 'local', location: '/tmp/alpha', addedAt: '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z' }, { id: 'b', name: 'Beta',  type: 'local', location: '/tmp/beta',  addedAt: '2025-01-02T00:00:00Z' }, { id: 'c', name: 'Gamma', type: 'local', location: '/tmp/gamma', addedAt: '2025-01-03T00:00:00Z' } ] }
test_cases.when: lib/store/projects.js readProjects() is invoked with HOME pointing at the tmpdir
test_cases.then: the returned object has .projects.length === 3 AND the names are ['Alpha','Beta','Gamma'] in that order AND no thrown exception
steps: set env HOME to tmpdir parent so ~/.aitri-hub resolves; OR use AITRI_HUB_DIR=tmpdir
test_cases.expected_result: All 3 legacy entries returned, order preserved, no crash on defaultInterface

test_cases.title: POST /api/projects on a legacy file produces a valid-JSON write that survives restart
preconditions: aitri-hub web can be spawned as a subprocess in the test
test_cases.given: AITRI_HUB_DIR=tmpdir containing a legacy projects.json with defaultInterface:'cli' and 1 project; aitri-hub web running on a free port
test_cases.when: a POST http://127.0.0.1:<port>/api/projects with body { name: 'Delta', type: 'local', location: '<existing tmpdir subdir>' } is sent, then the server is killed with SIGINT, then the server is started again with the same AITRI_HUB_DIR, then GET /api/projects is fetched
test_cases.then: the final GET response body projects.length === 2 AND the JSON file on disk parses with JSON.parse without throwing AND the file content contains 'Delta'
steps: seed tmpdir with legacy projects.json + 1 project
create a second tmpdir subdir as a valid project location
POST /api/projects with the Delta payload, assert 201
SIGINT server A, wait for exit
read projects.json, assert JSON.parse does not throw
test_cases.expected_result: Data round-trips through a restart; no migration step required

test_cases.title: projects.json without 'version' field still reads correctly
test_cases.given: a tmpdir with projects.json content { projects: [ { id: 'x', name: 'X', type: 'local', location: '/tmp/x', addedAt: '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z' } ] } (no version, no defaultInterface)
test_cases.when: readProjects() is invoked
test_cases.then: result.projects.length === 1 AND result.projects[0].name === 'X'
steps: write minimal projects.json (no version, no defaultInterface)
test_cases.expected_result: Minimal shape is accepted

test_cases.title: Missing projects.json does not crash the server
test_cases.given: AITRI_HUB_DIR=tmpdir with NO projects.json present
test_cases.when: aitri-hub web is started and GET /data/dashboard.json is requested
test_cases.then: server starts within 5s AND GET /data/dashboard.json returns status 200 with body parsable as JSON AND the parsed body has .projects === [] (an array)
steps: spawn aitri-hub web with AITRI_HUB_DIR=tmpdir, AITRI_HUB_PORT=<free>
test_cases.expected_result: Empty dashboard served, no crash, no migration prompt

test_cases.title: GET /api/projects returns projects array from localhost
test_cases.given: server running with AITRI_HUB_DIR=tmpdir containing projects.json with 2 entries
test_cases.when: fetch('http://127.0.0.1:<port>/api/projects') is called
test_cases.then: status === 200 AND body.projects.length === 2
test_cases.expected_result: Admin list endpoint returns registered projects

test_cases.title: POST-PUT-DELETE /api/projects round-trip succeeds from localhost
test_cases.given: server running with an empty projects.json and a tmpdir subdir <loc> that exists
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects { name:'E', type:'local', location:<loc> } → capture id; PUT /api/projects/<id> { name:'E2' }; DELETE /api/projects/<id>
test_cases.then: POST returns 201; PUT returns 200 with project.name === 'E2'; DELETE returns 204; subsequent GET /api/projects returns projects.length === 0
test_cases.expected_result: Full CRUD works — the admin API covers all registration operations

test_cases.title: Non-localhost peer on /api/projects receives 403
test_cases.given: server running; a test harness that can set req.socket.remoteAddress to '10.0.0.5' before the request handler runs (via an in-process http.createServer mock or a Node 20 test http server)
test_cases.when: a request is made to /api/projects with a simulated non-loopback remoteAddress
test_cases.then: status === 403 AND body parsed as JSON has .error === 'forbidden'
steps: instantiate the cmdWeb handler against a mock socket with remoteAddress='10.0.0.5'
test_cases.expected_result: Localhost guard rejects non-loopback callers

test_cases.title: POST /api/projects with non-existent path returns 400 path_not_found
test_cases.given: server running; a path that does not exist on disk, e.g. /tmp/definitely-does-not-exist-<random>
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects { name:'X', type:'local', location:'/tmp/definitely-does-not-exist-<random>' }
test_cases.then: status === 400 AND body.error === 'path_not_found'
steps: generate a random path that does not exist
test_cases.expected_result: Invalid location is rejected with a specific error code

test_cases.title: POST /api/projects with '..' in location returns 400 path_traversal
test_cases.given: server running
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects { name:'T', type:'local', location:'/tmp/../etc' }
test_cases.then: status === 400 AND body.error === 'path_traversal'
test_cases.expected_result: Traversal attempts are rejected with path_traversal

test_cases.title: Usage banner does not mention the word 'Docker' as a requirement
test_cases.given: the post-feature USAGE constant (captured from aitri-hub help stdout)
test_cases.when: the captured string is inspected
test_cases.then: the string does NOT contain the case-insensitive substring 'docker' (continuity with FR-004 — Docker must not be surfaced as a primary requirement)
test_cases.expected_result: CLI help does not mention Docker

test_cases.title: Clicking the empty-state CTA lands on /admin with a project-registration form visible
preconditions: aitri-hub web running against an empty ~/.aitri-hub/ (tmpdir)
test_cases.given: AITRI_HUB_DIR points to an empty tmpdir (no projects.json) and `aitri-hub web` is started on a free port
test_cases.when: a Playwright chromium page with viewport 1440x900 navigates to http://localhost:<port>/, waits for networkidle, and clicks the element matching `a[href='/admin']` whose text contains 'Add your first project'
test_cases.then: page.url() ends with '/admin' AND the admin page DOM contains exactly one element matching `input[name='name']` AND exactly one element matching `input[name='location']` AND at least one button whose text contains 'Add' or 'Register' or 'Save'
steps: launch Playwright chromium, newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 900 } })
teardown: SIGINT the server, remove tmpdir
test_cases.expected_result: CTA navigates to /admin and the registration form is present

test_cases.title: End-to-end project registration via the admin UI persists through a full page reload
preconditions: a real tmpdir exists on disk to use as a project location
test_cases.given: AITRI_HUB_DIR=<tmpdirA> (empty) and <tmpdirB> exists to serve as a valid project location; `aitri-hub web` is running on a free port
test_cases.when: a Playwright page navigates to http://localhost:<port>/admin, fills `input[name='name']` with 'E2E-Alpha', fills `input[name='location']` with <tmpdirB>, submits the form (click the submit button), waits for a success indicator (toast or list item), then page.reload() and waits for networkidle
test_cases.then: after reload, the page DOM contains the text 'E2E-Alpha' in at least one node AND GET /api/projects returns body.projects.length === 1 with projects[0].name === 'E2E-Alpha' AND the projects.json file on disk parses and contains 'E2E-Alpha'
test_cases.expected_result: A project registered via the admin UI is persisted to disk and remains visible after reload
